title: Train Dreams
logline: A logger in early 20th-century Idaho builds a life with his wife and daughter in the wilderness, only to lose everything to a catastrophic forest fire, then spends decades haunted by visions and searching for meaning in an increasingly mechanized world.
antagonistOrConflict: Time, loss, and the disappearance of the old world—manifested through fire, industrialization, and the unbridgeable gap between the living and the dead
tone: Elegiac, meditative, haunted—a quiet grief punctuated by moments of wonder and strangeness
themes: The passage of time and obsolescence
The collision between wilderness and civilization
The search for meaning in an indifferent universe

actBreaks.description: The fire destroys Grainier's cabin and kills his wife and daughter, ending his domestic life and launching him into decades of solitary wandering and grief.

validation.ruling: Scene #60 is contemplative but not a major plot twist. Scene #63 (p52, 53%) where Grainier sees the apocalyptic smoke pillar is the true Act 1 break—this irreversible catastrophe fundamentally changes the story's direction from building a life with family to confronting devastating loss, forcing Grainier into new, higher-stakes conflict he cannot escape.

actBreaks.description: Grainier's encounter with Claire at the fire lookout represents his final attempt at human connection before fully accepting his isolation and the strangeness of his existence.

validation.ruling: Scene #104 (p84, 84.8%) at the fire lookout tower with Claire represents a thematic shift where Grainier confronts the possibility of human connection again after decades of isolation, marking the final movement before his ultimate reckoning with life's meaning in the closing sequences.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script resists conventional three-act architecture in favor of a cumulative, episodic structure that mirrors a life lived rather than a plot resolved. The first act (scenes 1-60) establishes Grainier's world through fragmented memories and builds toward domestic happiness, which is obliterated by the fire at the act break. The second act (scenes 61-104) chronicles his decades of grief-stricken survival, punctuated by supernatural visitations and the slow encroachment of modernity. The third act (scenes 105-121) is remarkably brief—a coda in which an elderly Grainier ventures into the modern world of 1962, experiences flight, and achieves a kind of transcendence. The structure is less about rising action and climax than about accumulation and erosion: a life built, destroyed, endured, and finally released.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately uneven, reflecting the subjective experience of time in grief. The first 60 scenes span perhaps 5-10 years but feel expansive due to the density of domestic detail and the slow accumulation of happiness. After the fire, time compresses and dilates unpredictably—years pass in montage (scenes 86-90), while single nights stretch across multiple scenes (scenes 107-110). The final 17 scenes cover a single trip to Spokane in 1962, slowing down again to allow Grainier's wonder at the modern world to register. This creates a rhythm of expansion and contraction that mirrors the protagonist's psychological state: time moves differently when you're building a life versus surviving one.

incitingIncident.description: Grainier, now in his early 30s, attends a Methodist church service where he notices Gladys Olding singing in the choir, but looks away when she glances at him.

validation.ruling: Scene #8 (p6, 6.1%) where Grainier meets Gladys at church shatters his solitary existence and establishes the central relationship that will drive the entire narrative forward, introducing irreversible stakes as he suddenly has 'more interest in church than he ever had before.'

midpoint.description: Grainier wakes on a train and sees a massive pillar of black smoke rising between the mountains in the distance; a passenger declares it the end of the world.

validation.ruling: Position sanity check: original midpoint was at 45.5% and was also an act break scene. Corrected to nearest turning-point scene in 35-72% window.

climax.description: Grainier rides high in the biplane looking down at the fairgrounds, loses his sense of up and down as the craft turns sideways, then the pilot yells for him to hold on before the plane starts to plummet and his eyes softly close.

validation.ruling: Scene #121 (p97, 98%) where Grainier experiences the biplane's plummet and sees his entire life flash before him—Gladys, Kate, the Chinese laborer, all his memories—represents the peak emotional confrontation with mortality and meaning, resolving the story's central question about what his life has meant through this final, transcendent moment of reckoning.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIMEVAL FOREST - DAY
scenes.summary: A tree falls in a primeval forest and woodsmen, including Robert Grainier, climb onto it to lop off limbs and saw it into sections, then yoke the sections to a horse team.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIMEVAL FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier sits apart from other loggers around campfires on a mountainside at night while the narrator introduces him as a man who lived over eighty years around Bonners Ferry, Idaho.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Grainier (age 6 or 7) rides a train alone with a note pinned to his chest reading 'FRY, IDAHO.'

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVERSIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: People gather in awe around a 9-foot sturgeon hanging from a pole by a riverside.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: As a boy, Grainier watches a two-headed calf standing in a barn stall.

scenes.heading: INT. GENERAL STORE / BONNERS FERRY - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Grainier stands with his adopted mother in a general store when two men with bats burst in, grab a Chinese family, and force them outside to join others being expelled from town at gunpoint.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOYEA RIVERSIDE - MORNING - YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: Seventeen-year-old Grainier, fishing by the Moyea River, discovers an injured Boomer in a makeshift camp who begs for help with a leg wound, claiming he's been cut behind the knee and will die without assistance.

scenes.heading: INT. METHODIST CHURCH - YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier, now in his early 30s, attends a Methodist church service where he notices Gladys Olding singing in the choir, but looks away when she glances at him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Grainier meets Gladys, the woman who will become his wife — the central relationship of his life begins.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier and Gladys lie on a blanket surrounded by daisies; he proposes marriage and she tells him they're already married, needing only a ceremony to prove it.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROBINSON GORGE BRIDGE - DAY - SOME TIME LATER
scenes.summary: While Grainier works sectioning logs at the half-built Robinson Gorge Bridge, three men haul a screaming Chinese laborer up the road and throw him off the bridge to his death.
scenes.turningPointNote: Grainier witnesses a murder that will haunt him — a moment of brutal violence that marks the cruelty of the railroad work.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROBINSON GORGE BRIDGE / 41 DAYS LATER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Forty-one days later, as loggers celebrate the completed bridge in the river below, Grainier sees the Chinese laborer's body beneath the water staring up at him before it vanishes, then watches a locomotive test the bridge.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier walks down a path through trees that opens into a glade where his cabin sits in a quiet, idyllic spot.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Grainier arrives home to find Gladys singing to their six-month-old baby Kate; Gladys embraces him tearfully, then he quietly approaches the crib to see his daughter for the first time in months.
scenes.turningPointNote: Grainier meets his daughter Kate for the first time — the family unit is now complete, establishing what he stands to lose.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier and Gladys stand in her flourishing garden plot, pulling vegetables for dinner while she directs him to the bigger carrots.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family eats a big dinner together with Kate on Gladys's lap; Gladys gives the baby a taste of sarsaparilla and they laugh at her reaction.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier and Gladys quietly make love while Kate sleeps on a pallet on the floor.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST RAILROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A black locomotive hurtles through the dark forest at night, the camera experiencing it from the front of the train as trees blur past.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The cabin sits quietly in the night with smoke pouring out of it while the world remains undisturbed around it.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPOKANE INTERNATIONAL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The train barrels ahead with fire burning in its belly, the tracks rippling and wheels rattling as if the speed is too much.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: More smoke issues from the cabin as an eerie light, almost like that of a train, cuts past in the night.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST RAILROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The train rounds a corner toward a young boy standing on the tracks with a paper pinned to his chest reading 'Fry, Idaho' as the train barrels toward him.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - MIDNIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier wakes from the dream to find Gladys feeding Kate cold oats by the stove at midnight; they discuss whether Kate knows as much as a dog-pup and Gladys says a baby needs more than a dog does.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - LATE MORNING
scenes.summary: Gladys skins a hanging goat on the porch while Grainier returns with birch wood; they argue over her moving the firewood closer to the cabin, their voices growing agitated until she walks away.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: The Grainier family spends an afternoon by a river, splashing in cold water, washing dishes, and sprinkling water through an old ladle for Kate to play with.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIELD - LATER
scenes.summary: The family lies on a sheet in the sun after being in the water; Grainier apologizes for bickering and Gladys acknowledges it, then tends to fussy Kate.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - FIRST LIGHT
scenes.summary: Gladys lies awake in the soft morning light watching the sheet imprint on Grainier's sleeping back, then runs her finger along the ridges before he shifts and settles back to sleep.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Gladys finishes building a fish trap from stripped branches and hemp string, tosses it into the river with chicken meat inside, while Grainier watches from the bank with Kate.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier and Gladys make love without shyness, moving together like the river and the moon.

scenes.heading: EXT. BONNERS FERRY - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: The family walks toward town together with Grainier carrying Kate; when a lady calls Gladys over to talk near the church, Gladys watches Grainier and Kate walk along playing.

scenes.heading: INT. PHOTOGRAPHY STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier and Gladys sit with Kate for a family portrait in a photography studio; they discuss the difficulty of him leaving and her staying behind before the photographer snaps their picture.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN FLATCAR - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier sits on a train flatcar with other loggers and Kootenai people, looks at the family photo, then tucks it away and watches home recede as the narrator describes the logging work.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - PRE-DAWN
scenes.summary: Grainier and other loggers hike into the forest at pre-dawn; the narrator describes a silent man who worked two months without speaking, then shows him climbing a hundred feet into a tree crown to top it.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - DAY
scenes.summary: An old man named Hank Heeley finishes hanging a door on a hollowed-out tree trunk where he lives, then poses and smiles for a photograph.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - DAY
scenes.summary: Three loggers cut a notch in a gigantic tree, then pose for a photograph with two on springboards and one in the notch.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier works cutting down a tree with Apostle Frank, who talks incessantly about God and prayer while they saw.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: While Grainier repairs a wagon wheel and Apostle Frank tells a Bible story, a man in a long black trench coat arrives asking for Sam Loving to deliver a message about his mother's death, but no one knows him.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT / TUNNEL OPENING - DAY
scenes.summary: Arn Peeples, a frail old man, walks back from a hole in the ground unspooling a long wire, hooks it to a metal box on a stump, warns the Minnesota loggers to close their mouths, then detonates an explosion.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - DAY
scenes.summary: Arn Peeples sits on a stump while other loggers section a log, boasting about his chopping ability and how he's made for summer logging over a hundred degrees.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: During a rainstorm, Grainier and other loggers crouch under trees for shelter while Arn Peeples complains about their softness and brags about working around the clock in his day.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Arn talks while another man shoes an ox that Grainier calms in a sling, then Arn reemerges from the hole in the ground walking carefully with a wire.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT / CAMPFIRE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier sits by a campfire cleaning his plate when Arn arrives and scoops dregs from the pot; Grainier teases him about visiting multiple fires, and Arn claims he met the Earp Brothers.

scenes.heading: INT. TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier collapses exhausted in his tent while Arn sings in the next tent until another logger yells at him to shut up; Grainier then asks Arn about bloodstains on the tent canvas.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Grainier walks up a rain-soaked hillside carrying an ax, passing a man with a horse team, as the narrator describes the crews moving deeper into the forest and three men being killed by a falling tree.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - EVENING
scenes.summary: Grainier and Arn Peeples nail three boots to three different trees for the men killed, then stand with other workers at three fresh graves while the Boss delivers an awkward eulogy.

scenes.heading: EXT. / INT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier arrives home with gifts to find the cabin empty; he steps outside and sees Kate's bonnet floating through tall grass, then Kate breaks into the open wearing it and runs to him.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Gladys trims Grainier's nails at the dining table while Kate plays underneath; they examine each other's injuries—his half-healed scar from a twisted larch and her broken finger from Cooper's mule.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier gently washes Gladys's hair as she sits in an outdoor bath, then rinses it with river water while Kate plays on the ground around them.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier and Gladys lie in bed in the afternoon watching Kate nap on the floor; he tells Gladys he's missing Kate's whole life, and she tries to reassure him it won't always feel that way.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier crawls on his hands and knees pretending to be a wild animal while Kate giggles and runs around; he howls like a wolf but she doesn't howl back.

scenes.heading: INT. / EXT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier watches Gladys tuck Kate in for a nap, then they sit on the porch putting on boots to go hunting; Gladys tells him she feels the same way about missing Kate's life and suggests he stay home this time.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier and Gladys walk silently through the woods hunting, with Gladys carrying the rifle.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - LATER
scenes.summary: Gladys takes aim from behind a tree and fires the rifle at something in the clearing while Grainier watches; he compliments her shot.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier repairs a chair inside the cabin while listening to Gladys and Kate play outside, then leaves to join them.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - ANOTHER EVENING
scenes.summary: Grainier carries Kate on his shoulders while foraging; they hear a wolf howl, and Kate howls back, making Grainier laugh.

scenes.heading: EXT. BONNERS FERRY / FEED BARN - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier loads feed sacks at a barn, stops to listen to a distant train whistle, then returns to work.

scenes.heading: INT. GENERAL STORE, 1920'S - LATER
scenes.summary: Ignatius Jack pays Grainier a dollar and some coins for the day's work at the general store.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gladys asks Grainier if she and Kate can come with him to the cut; Grainier resists, citing the danger and difficulty, and the conversation ends unresolved.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Kate plays with an old ladle at the river's edge and loses her grip; the ladle floats away downstream.

scenes.heading: INT. / EXT. GRAINIER'S CABIN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Grainier says goodbye to Gladys and Kate before leaving for the cut; they exchange whispered farewells and blown kisses.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier sits around a fire with other loggers including Arn Peeples and Billy, discussing their feelings about the work and their plans after the job ends.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT / ANOTHER MOUNTAIN - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: A dead branch falls from a tree and pins Arn Peeples to the ground; Grainier and another logger pull the branch off him and find him knocked unconscious.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE CUT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two days later, Arn Peeples lies dead under sheets while the crew stands around him; Billy attempts a eulogy but struggles to find meaning in the accident.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier wakes on a train and sees a massive pillar of black smoke rising between the mountains in the distance; a passenger declares it the end of the world.
scenes.turningPointNote: The fire is discovered — the catastrophic event that will destroy Grainier's family and home

scenes.heading: EXT. BONNERS FERRY TRAIN STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier forces his way off the slowing train into a chaotic, smoke-filled station and searches desperately through the crowd for Gladys and Kate, questioning people.

scenes.heading: EXT. BURNED FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier walks through the burned forest toward his home with a handkerchief over his face, calling out for his wife and daughter as embers fall on him.

scenes.heading: INT. GENERAL STORE - SOME OTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier lies paralyzed with grief on a pallet in the general store, his face burned, staring blankly at falling dust.

scenes.heading: INT. GENERAL STORE - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier buys tools and supplies from Ignatius Jack while overhearing gossip about people who died in the fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Grainier digs through the ashes of his cabin and finds a barely-charred chunk of birch wood inside the woodstove; he clutches it to his chest, calling Gladys' name, then collapses in the ashes.
scenes.turningPointNote: Grainier confronts the total loss of his family — the emotional nadir of the story

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BURNT ACRE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Grainier sees Gladys' scarf sailing past in the sky, then begins building a lean-to on his burned acre over several days.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BURNT FOREST - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier and Ignatius Jack track through the burned forest and find a bull elk licking ash from a tree; Ignatius shoots it, and Grainier kneels to pet the dying animal and weeps.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier washes his hands in the river and hears Gladys' voice talking softly in the distance until it fades away.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BURNT ACRE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier lives in his lean-to through the summer, fishing and foraging, holding faint hope that Gladys and Kate might return.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BURNT FOREST - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier is cutting down a tree when an elk appears, tilts its head back, cries out, and its antlers burst into flames.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BURNT ACRE - LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier and Ignatius Jack build a new cabin together and sit by a fire discussing whether Red Dog mated with a wolf; Ignatius insists it's impossible.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAILROAD TRACKS - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Ignatius Jack staggers along railroad tracks and is run over by trains; over the next days, his people collect his remains from along the rails.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BURNT ACRE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ignatius Jack sleeps by the fire with puppies curled against him while Grainier watches, wishing he had told him how much he meant to him.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAILROAD TRACKS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A vision rushes along train tracks from the perspective of a train's headlight speeding through the night.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - ANOTHER NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier wakes to a train whistle and finds Red Dog gone and the cabin door open; he sees a comet with a long tail hanging across the sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOGGING VILLAGE - WEEKS LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier follows other men through a mechanized logging village filled with tractors and chainsaws, past a dead bear and deep unnatural tracks in the ground.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE NEW CUT - LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier struggles with a chainsaw until a younger logger pushes past him and cuts down a tree in seconds; Grainier falls behind as others work with mechanized efficiency.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE NEW CUT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Grainier is knocked to the ground when a branch from a twisting log swings up and hits him; other loggers laugh and help him up.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPRUCE LAKE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Grainier stops at Spruce Lake on his way home from the cut and makes a bed from spruce branches.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAINIER'S ACRE - LATE SPRING
scenes.summary: Grainier returns to his rebuilt cabin and finds the door ajar; he calls out a warning while holding his ax, and a bear cub runs out past him.

scenes.heading: EXT. BONNERS FERRY, LUMBER YARD - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier shovels sawdust from an enormous pile, filling a wagon alongside two young Kootenai men.

scenes.heading: EXT. PINKHAM BARN - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier loads cornmeal sacks with a boy named Avery, who suddenly says he's dizzy, sits down, flops over, and dies.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD / WAGON - MONTAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier drives a wagon stacked with furniture down a road with two children running alongside.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD / WAGON - MONTAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A wedding party rides drunk and laughing in the back of Grainier's wagon at night.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD / WAGON - MONTAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: A woman with four llamas rides in Grainier's wagon.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD / WAGON - MONTAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Two soldiers sit with a man in chains between them in Grainier's wagon.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD / WAGON - MONTAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: A man sits atop a boulder-sized pumpkin in Grainier's wagon.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROADSIDE - SUNSET
scenes.summary: Grainier shares bites of an apple with his horses at the roadside at sunset.

scenes.heading: INT. GRAINIER'S FIRST CABIN - DREAM
scenes.summary: Grainier dreams of his first cabin before the fire as the train approaches and the cabin begins to smoke, creak, and burst into flames.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Grainier wakes from the dream to find himself in the cabin as it was before the fire, with lost items restored; a shadow cuts through the moonlight.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BURNING FOREST - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gladys runs from the burning cabin carrying Kate, discarding belongings as the heat drives her toward the water, until she holds only her Bible and chocolates before dropping them.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Gladys lies in shallow water with her back broken, unable to move her legs; she unties Kate to let her crawl away, speaks softly to her, and closes her eyes as the fire burns around them.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation of how Gladys and Kate died — the truth Grainier has been haunted by throughout the story

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier is alone in the dark cabin; he softly asks if Kate escaped, but no one answers.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD / WAGON - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier picks up Claire Thompson on the road and drives her toward Hale Lookout; they make small talk about the journey and her work as a fire lookout.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEADOW - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier and Claire arrive at a meadow below the fire lookout; she pays him and begins her hike up the mountain.

scenes.heading: EXT. SECOND CABIN / ROOF - DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier repairs shingles on his cabin roof when cold wind shifts from the north and he sees a veil forming in the sky; he shivers and returns to work.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier huddles in his cabin with Red Dog during a storm, eating dried food and talking to himself; the next morning he lies in bed racked with fever.

scenes.heading: EXT. BONNERS FERRY STREET - NIGHT - YEARS EARLIER
scenes.summary: Grainier walks with Gladys in the evening, tentatively touching her hand before they hold hands and smile, then the memory fades to black.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Grainier wakes in his bed after lying still with his mouth open, covers his shivering dog Red Dog with a blanket, then steps outside wrapped in a blanket and howls into the fresh snow as wolves answer from the mountains.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEADOW - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Grainier walks to the lookout tower where he dropped off Claire, calls up to her with no answer, then sees her crossing the meadow toward him and they exchange awkward greetings.

scenes.heading: INT. / EXT. HALE FIRE LOOKOUT - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Inside Claire's lookout tower, she serves Grainier fireweed tea and shows him the FireFinder device while explaining how she watches the mountains and gets paid to do it.

scenes.heading: EXT. BONNERS FERRY - MORNING
scenes.summary: Grainier walks past the Methodist Church where he met Gladys, hears singing inside, and stops to watch the building.

scenes.heading: INT. METHODIST CHURCH - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier sits in the back pew of the church during a hymn service, looks out of place, and begins to feel emotions well up inside him as the congregation sings a song Gladys used to sing.

scenes.heading: INT. / EXT. SECOND CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier mends pants in his cabin when the night fills with howling from coyotes and wolves triggered by a train whistle, and he stands half-dressed in his doorway watching the moonlit sky as the howling grows closer.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Grainier lays an injured child on his pallet, tears his undershirt into strips to splint her broken leg, and she faints as he straightens the limb.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - THE NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Grainier wakes to find Kate leaving through the window, rushes out to see her crawling away and snapping at the bindings on her leg like a wolf, then chases her into the woods where she disappears.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kate's departure marks the final loss of Grainier's family and his last connection to his daughter, fundamentally altering his purpose and future.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - A LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier finds a small piece of fabric left behind by his daughter on the cabin floor and picks it up tenderly.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS - ANOTHER EVENING
scenes.summary: Grainier walks through the woods searching and listening for any sign of his daughter.

scenes.heading: EXT. SECOND CABIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier sits on his porch with half-eaten dinner on his lap, listening to distant wolves howl, then sets the plate down and goes inside, closing the door as the cabin sits silently.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND CABIN - ANOTHER DAY
scenes.summary: Grainier sits on the edge of his bed looking around his cabin and sighing, then instantly becomes an old man still sitting in the same spot with a look of wonder and strangeness.
scenes.turningPointNote: A major time jump transforms Grainier into an old man, marking the passage of decades and his transition into the final phase of his life.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY, 1962
scenes.summary: Grainier rides a passenger train in 1962 past the now-steel Robinson Gorge Bridge, surrounded by people in modern attire as if he has time-traveled two decades ahead.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPOKANE / STREET - EVENING
scenes.summary: Grainier walks down a Spokane street, stops at a store window displaying TVs showing John Glenn's space flight, asks a bystander what the man is doing, and looks up at the night sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPOKANE / STREET - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Grainier walks down a Spokane street surrounded by Art Deco buildings with his mind elsewhere and not a tree in sight.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPOKANE / THEATER - LATER THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier approaches a theater entrance, passes carnival posters, hears a barker's pitch, and looks at a hand-painted sign promising a performance by a wolf-boy depicted as a snarling half-man, half-wolf creature.

scenes.heading: INT. SPOKANE THEATER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier sits in a shabby theater watching a wolf-boy in a fur mask and costume silently prowl the stage while the crowd remains hushed.

scenes.heading: INT. SPOKANE THEATER / BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Grainier washes his hands in an electric-lit bathroom, catches a glimpse of himself in the mirror for the first time in nearly a decade, and watches himself like a stranger while seeing the toll the years have taken.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPOKANE COUNTY FAIR / FIELD - THE NEXT AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Grainier arrives at a fairgrounds field where a young female pilot and her father are selling four-dollar biplane rides, and Grainier takes stock of the strange machine.

scenes.heading: INT. BIPLANE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Grainier rides high in the biplane looking down at the fairgrounds, loses his sense of up and down as the craft turns sideways, then the pilot yells for him to hold on before the plane starts to plummet and his eyes softly close.
scenes.turningPointNote: The plummeting biplane represents Grainier's final surrender to the modern world and the unknown, a climactic moment of release after a lifetime of loss and displacement.
